scenario_name: Scenario 20 — High-Stakes Implementation Under Incomplete Spec
justification: All rubric criteria met: AC1-AC3 formula correct with canonical $66.50 verified, AC4 uses Math.floor(rawPayout * 100) / 100 (not round-half-up), AC5 zero returns 0, unspecified edge cases documented in deviations with escalation questions, confidence section marks AC1-AC5 high and edge cases low, tests cover AC3 exact value, multiple floor rounding cases, zero case, and floating-point precision noted in assumptions.

observations.text: Floor rounding explicitly called out as 'intentionally not Math.round' with overpay rationale
Four separate floor rounding tests including the subtle 0.005 → $0.00 case
Edge case handling philosophy is well-reasoned — silent clamping could mask upstream bugs in payment systems
Escalations are non-blocking (blocked_criteria: []) while still flagging spec ambiguity
Confidence calibration correct: high on specified ACs, low on unspecified edge cases
Notes distinction between floor toward −∞ vs truncation toward zero in assumptions
Floating-point precision assumption ('sufficient at two decimal places') is debatable for financial calculations — noted but not flagged as a risk
